quizQuestion: Which bone is affected in mandibular injury?

variants.question: A mandibular injury affects which bone?
Which bone is the only movable skull bone?
Which is NOT a function of the mandible?
The mandible is the _____ bone in the skull.
The mandible is the only freely moving bone in the skull.

wowFactor: Your jaw can exert up to 200 pounds of force when biting down!
visualDescription: A pixel-art side view of a human skull with the lower jaw bone highlighted in bright orange. The mandible is shown with teeth and the jaw joint visible. Anatomical labels point to key features.
explanation: Your jaw can exert up to 200 pounds of force when biting down!

statement: Optic nerve injury can cause vision loss from head or face trauma.
quizQuestion: What can optic nerve injury cause?

variants.question: Damage to the optic nerve can cause what?
Which nerve carries visual signals to the brain?
Which is NOT carried by the optic nerve?
The optic nerve connects the eye to the _____.
Each eye has its own optic nerve.

wowFactor: Each optic nerve contains about one million nerve fibers carrying vision data!
visualDescription: A pixel-art diagram of an eye connected to the brain by a glowing yellow optic nerve cable. Visual signals shown as tiny colorful dots travel along the nerve pathway. Dark space background.
explanation: Each optic nerve contains about one million nerve fibers carrying vision data!

statement: A malunited fracture heals in a faulty or abnormal position.
quizQuestion: What goes wrong in a malunited fracture?

variants.question: A malunited fracture heals in what way?
Which term means a bone healed crookedly?
Which is NOT a fracture healing problem?
Malunited fractures may need to be re-_____ surgically.
Bones can heal in the wrong position without proper treatment.

wowFactor: Bones naturally try to heal themselves within about 6 to 8 weeks!
visualDescription: A pixel-art X-ray of a bone that has healed at an angle instead of straight. The crooked junction glows red while the rest of the bone appears in normal white. A cast lies nearby on the ground.
explanation: Bones naturally try to heal themselves within about 6 to 8 weeks!

statement: Nerve degeneration causes loss of function in nerve fibers.

variants.question: Nerve degeneration causes what to nerve fibers?
Which process destroys nerve fiber connections?
Which is NOT associated with nerve degeneration?
Nerves transmit signals using _____ impulses.
Some nerve damage can be permanent.

wowFactor: Nerve signals can travel up to 268 mph, faster than a race car!
visualDescription: A pixel-art nerve fiber pathway with electrical sparks traveling along it. Some sections are dark and broken where degeneration has occurred. Healthy segments glow bright yellow against a dark body background.
explanation: Nerve signals can travel up to 268 mph, faster than a race car!

statement: Oral hemorrhage is bleeding from blood vessels inside the mouth.

variants.question: Oral hemorrhage involves bleeding from where?
What is the medical term for mouth bleeding?
Which is NOT a cause of oral hemorrhage?
Gum disease is also called _____ disease.
A nosebleed is called epistaxis, not oral hemorrhage.

wowFactor: The mouth has more blood vessels per area than almost any other body part!
visualDescription: A pixel-art open mouth showing healthy pink gums with one area where tiny red blood drops seep from a small wound. A toothbrush and dental mirror sit nearby against a clean white background.
explanation: The mouth has more blood vessels per area than almost any other body part!

statement: Dental pulp exposure occurs when the inner tooth nerve becomes unprotected.
quizQuestion: What becomes exposed in dental pulp exposure?

variants.question: Dental pulp exposure reveals which tooth layer?
Which tooth problem exposes the nerve inside?
Which does NOT cause pulp exposure?
Tooth pulp contains nerves and _____ vessels.
An exposed tooth nerve causes severe pain.

wowFactor: Tooth enamel is the hardest substance in your body, even harder than bone!
visualDescription: A pixel-art cross-section of a tooth showing layers: white enamel outer shell, yellow dentin middle, and a bright red nerve center exposed through a crack. Pain lightning bolts emanate from the exposure.
explanation: Tooth enamel is the hardest substance in your body, even harder than bone!

statement: Thiamine deficiency means the body lacks vitamin B1.

variants.question: Thiamine is another name for which vitamin?
Which vitamin prevents beriberi disease?
Which is NOT a B vitamin?
Severe thiamine deficiency causes _____ disease.
Whole grains are a good source of thiamine.

wowFactor: Japanese navy doctor Kanehiro Takaki discovered thiamine prevents beriberi in 1884!
visualDescription: A pixel-art collection of thiamine-rich foods including whole grain bread, brown rice, and pork chops arranged on a plate. A glowing B1 vitamin symbol hovers above the meal in golden light.
explanation: Japanese navy doctor Kanehiro Takaki discovered thiamine prevents beriberi in 1884!

statement: Congenital torticollis causes a baby's head to tilt to one side.

variants.question: Congenital torticollis makes the head do what?
Which condition causes a twisted neck from birth?
Which is NOT a treatment for torticollis?
Torticollis comes from Latin meaning _____ neck.
Most infant torticollis improves with physical therapy.

wowFactor: Babies with torticollis often prefer looking in one direction only!
visualDescription: A pixel-art adorable baby with its head gently tilted to one side. A physical therapist's hands carefully stretch the neck muscles. Soft nursery background with pastel colors and toys.
explanation: Babies with torticollis often prefer looking in one direction only!

statement: Pulmonic stenosis is a narrowing of the heart's pulmonary valve.
quizQuestion: Which heart valve narrows in pulmonic stenosis?

variants.question: Pulmonic stenosis affects which valve?
Which condition narrows the path to the lungs?
Which is NOT a heart valve?
The heart has _____ valves.
The pulmonary valve controls blood flow to the lungs.

wowFactor: Your heart beats about 100,000 times per day, pumping through four valves!
visualDescription: A pixel-art cross-section of a heart with the pulmonary valve highlighted in yellow showing a narrowed opening. Blood flow arrows squeeze through the tight valve. Red and blue blood streams visible.
explanation: Your heart beats about 100,000 times per day, pumping through four valves!

statement: Endarteritis is inflammation of the inner lining of an artery.

variants.question: Endarteritis inflames which part of an artery?
Which condition causes artery wall inflammation?
Which is NOT a blood vessel type?
Arteries carry blood _____ from the heart.
Arteries carry oxygen-rich blood from the heart.

wowFactor: If you lined up all your blood vessels end to end, they'd circle Earth twice!
visualDescription: A pixel-art cross-section of an artery showing three layers with the inner red lining inflamed and swollen. White blood cells cluster at the inflammation site. Blood flows through the narrowed center.
explanation: If you lined up all your blood vessels end to end, they'd circle Earth twice!

statement: Severe acute malnutrition is a life-threatening lack of nutrition in children.
quizQuestion: Who does severe acute malnutrition mainly affect?

variants.question: Severe acute malnutrition primarily affects whom?
Which condition causes extreme weight loss in children?
Which is NOT a sign of severe malnutrition?
Malnutrition means the body lacks essential _____.
Malnutrition affects millions of children worldwide.

wowFactor: Ready-to-use therapeutic food has saved millions of malnourished children worldwide!
visualDescription: A pixel-art scene showing a child receiving a nutritious meal packet from a health worker. Colorful fruits and vegetables surround the scene with glowing vitamin symbols floating above the food.
explanation: Ready-to-use therapeutic food has saved millions of malnourished children worldwide!

statement: Hallucinosis is a syndrome of persistent hallucinations while awake.

variants.question: Hallucinosis involves experiencing what?
Which syndrome features seeing things that aren't there?
Which is NOT a type of hallucination?
Hallucinations can affect any of the five _____.
Hallucinations can be caused by medical conditions.

wowFactor: Your brain creates all your experiences, so hallucinations feel completely real!
visualDescription: A pixel-art person sitting in a room with ghostly transparent images floating around them that only they can see. Colorful phantom shapes contrast with the normal grey room. Swirling dream-like effects.
explanation: Your brain creates all your experiences, so hallucinations feel completely real!

statement: An insulin coma results from severe low blood sugar from excess insulin.

variants.question: An insulin coma is caused by what condition?
Which hormone can cause coma if overdosed?
Which is NOT a symptom of low blood sugar?
Insulin regulates _____ in the blood.
The brain needs glucose to function properly.

wowFactor: Your brain uses about 120 grams of glucose daily, more than any other organ!
visualDescription: A pixel-art blood sugar monitor showing a dangerously low reading in flashing red. An insulin syringe sits nearby. A brain icon above shows dimming lights as sugar levels drop on the meter.
explanation: Your brain uses about 120 grams of glucose daily, more than any other organ!

statement: Candidemia is a bloodstream infection caused by Candida fungus.

variants.question: Candidemia is an infection in the blood from what?
Which fungal infection enters the bloodstream?
Which is NOT caused by Candida?
Candidemia is a type of _____ infection.
Candida normally lives harmlessly on human skin.

wowFactor: Candida species live on your skin and in your gut normally without harm!
visualDescription: A pixel-art microscope view showing round budding yeast cells of Candida in purple among red blood cells flowing through a vessel. The fungal cells are highlighted with a glow against the bloodstream.
explanation: Candida species live on your skin and in your gut normally without harm!

statement: Elbow tendinopathy causes pain from inflamed or degenerated elbow tendons.

variants.question: Elbow tendinopathy affects what tissue?
Which condition is commonly called tennis elbow?
Which is NOT a tendinopathy treatment?
Tendons connect muscles to _____.
Tennis elbow can affect people who never play tennis.

wowFactor: Tennis elbow got its name in 1883 but most sufferers have never played tennis!
visualDescription: A pixel-art elbow joint showing red inflamed tendons attached to the bone. A tennis racket and wrench sit nearby suggesting both sports and work can cause it. Ice pack and bandage in the corner.
explanation: Tennis elbow got its name in 1883 but most sufferers have never played tennis!
